subject: Biology
title: Producers, Consumers, and Decomposers
description: A 60-second lesson on Producers, Consumers, and Decomposers.

hook.text: Every organism in an ecosystem plays a role: make food, eat food, or break down the dead.

buildup.text: Producers like plants and algae capture sunlight and convert it into chemical energy.

discovery.text: Consumers eat other organisms. Herbivores eat plants; carnivores eat animals; omnivores eat both.

twist.text: Decomposers are the unsung heroes—fungi and bacteria recycle dead matter back into nutrients.

climax.text: Without decomposers, dead material would pile up and nutrients would be locked away forever.

punchline.text: Death feeds life. That's the circle.

quiz.question: What role do decomposers play in an ecosystem?
options: They recycle dead matter into nutrients
They hunt and eat other animals
